title: Kisses (ft. bbyclose)

chord_progression.implied_by: bass line — no block chord instrument; harmony implied by Organ Bass, FM Bass, and Pluck melody
chord_progression.theory_notes: D natural minor: D, E, F, G, A, Bb, C. The bass-implied chord loop i→bIII→bVI→iv (Dm→F→Bb→Gm) is one of the most emotional progressions in soul, R&B, and UK garage. Each chord is 4 beats (one bar), creating a 4-bar cycle. Dm = dark home. F major (bIII) = unexpected brightness, lifts the mood. Bb major (bVI) = the warmth peak — major chord on the flat 6th is the most 'soulful' moment. Gm (iv) = return toward darkness before the loop resets. The Pluck melody outlines these changes note by note: D→A over Dm, F→C over F, Bb→F over Bb, G→D over Gm. This is harmony through melody, not chords — very BL3SS.

structure.description: Vocal only (bbyclose). No instruments. Sets the emotional tone completely dry — voice and silence.
Organ Bass enters — plays the full chord loop (Dm→F→Bb→Gm). No kick yet. Establishes harmonic identity.
Kick intro (sparse pattern, not 4-on-the-floor yet). Open Hat 2 enters. Snare 2 with ghost notes. FX 1 audio at bars 17–25.
FM Bass enters (4-on-the-floor bass drive). Kick goes to full 4-on-the-floor (every beat). FX 2 transition hits.
Clap, Hat (16th-note grid), Open Hat 1 (off-beats), Pluck 1 melody all enter. Full arrangement. FX 1 audio at bar 33.
Snare 1 fills (16th-note roll) at bar 40 for transition into bar 41.
Saws 1 enters — adds harmonic colour to the mix. FM Bass + Saws together = warmer, fuller drop. FX 1 at bar 41.
Snare fill again at bar 48 transition.

FM Bass drops. Organ Bass returns (same as bars 9–16). Saws 1 continues for harmonic interest. Pads enter: Pad 3 (bar 49), Pad 1 (bar 53), Pad 2 (bar 56) — layered atmospheric pads building tension. Open Hat 2 re-enters. FX 3 and FX 4 swells. FX 5 drone (bar 57–64).
Full arrangement returns: FM Bass, Kick (4-on-the-floor), Clap, Hat, Open Hat 1+3, Saws 1+2, Lead (= same notes as Pluck). FX 1 at bar 65. Maximum energy.
FX 2 and FX 3 fade. Vocal tail out.

tracks.name: Kick
tracks.role: Sparse pattern bars 17–24 (intro), full 4-on-the-floor (every beat) from bar 25. C4, velocity 127.

tracks.name: Clap
tracks.role: Clap on beats 2 and 4 (A#3, positions 1.0, 3.0, 5.0, 7.0 per 2-bar phrase). Velocity 127. Enters bar 33.

tracks.name: Hat
tracks.role: 16th-note hi-hat grid with humanised velocity pattern [93, 110, 127, 113 cycling]. C4. Creates a swinging groove feel despite no official swing setting.

tracks.name: Open Hat 1
tracks.role: Open hat on every off-beat (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5 per bar). C4, velocity 100. Enters bar 33.

tracks.name: Open Hat 2
tracks.role: Same off-beat open hat — active in build (bars 17–24) and break (bars 49–64)

tracks.name: Open Hat 3
tracks.role: Third open hat layer in final drop (bars 57–64, 65+)

tracks.name: Snare 1
tracks.role: 16th-note snare roll fills at bar transitions (bars 40–41, 48–49, 72–73, 80–81)

tracks.name: Snare 2
tracks.role: Humanised snare with ghost notes — active in build and break. Pattern: strong hits on 2+4, ghost notes at 16th positions between.

tracks.name: Organ Bass
tracks.role: Bass in intro and break (bars 9–17, 17–24, 49–57, 57–64). Plays walking Dm→F→Bb→G pattern with 16th-note articulations. Note range: C3–G3 with D4 accent octave.

tracks.name: FM Bass
tracks.role: Main drop bass (bars 25–49, 65–81). Plays same Dm→F→Bb→Gm root pattern but with tighter 16th-note articulation (each note 0.49 beat duration, 0.5 beat offset). FM synthesis — brighter, more metallic than the Organ Bass.

tracks.name: Pluck 1
tracks.role: Main melodic hook — outlines chord progression note by note: D4→A4 (Dm), F3→C5 (F), Bb3→F4 (Bb), E4→G3→D4 (Gm). Enters bar 33. Creates the track's signature melodic identity.

tracks.name: Saws 1
tracks.role: Saw synth — dense 16th-note sustained notes outlining A3, C4, F3 (Am/F chord fragments from D minor). Enters bar 41. Adds harmonic warmth and weight.

tracks.name: Saws 2
tracks.role: Second saw layer (D3 drone), enters bar 65 for extra density in final drop

tracks.name: Lead
tracks.role: Lead synth in final drop (bars 65–81) — same melody as Pluck 1 but different timbre. Doubles the melodic hook.

tracks.name: Pad 1
tracks.role: Soft pad — D4 and A4 sustained (bars 53–64). Adds harmonic warmth in break.

tracks.name: Pad 2
tracks.role: Sub-pad at D3 (bars 56–64)

tracks.name: Pad 3
tracks.role: Sustained D3 pad (bars 49–61). Layered with Pad 1 and 2 for break texture.

tracks.name: Vocals
tracks.role: bbyclose vocal — entire track (bars 1–83). Starts dry for 8 bars before any instruments.

tracks.name: FX 1
tracks.role: Rhythmic FX hits at drop downbeats (bars 17, 25, 33, 41, 65, 73)

tracks.name: FX 2
tracks.role: Transition FX — short hits at section changes throughout

tracks.name: FX 3
tracks.role: Sweep/riser FX in break (bars 49–51) and outro (bars 81–83)

tracks.name: FX 4
tracks.role: Sustained build FX in break (bars 49–53)

tracks.name: FX 5
tracks.role: Drone/texture in second half of break (bars 57–64)

kick.pattern: 4-on-the-floor — beats 1, 2, 3, 4 (every beat) from bar 25. Sparse intro pattern bars 17–24.

hat.pattern: 16th notes with cycling velocity pattern: [93, 110, 127, 113] repeating every 4 16th notes

bass_pattern.key_notes: D natural minor: D=root, F=b3, G=4, Bb(A#)=b6, C=b7
bass_pattern.description: Bass walks a 4-bar loop (16 beats) through the D minor chord tones: 4 beats on D, 4 beats on F, 4 beats on Bb, 4 beats on G. Each root note is played with 16th-note articulation (3 quick notes per chord = walking triplet feel) plus a D4 octave accent on beat 4 of each bar. Creates rhythmic momentum through rapid repeated notes rather than long holds.
bass_pattern.organ_bass_bar1: D3×3 (beats 0-3) → D4 accent (beat 3.5) → F3 next bar
bass_pattern.fm_bass_technique: Same root notes but with each note offset by 0.5 beats and duration 0.49 beats — creates a slight syncopated 'pushing ahead' feel. FM synthesis gives sharper attack than Organ Bass.
bass_pattern.theory_note: The bass creates the full harmony with no chord instrument — listener implies Dm, F, Bb, Gm from the single notes. This is economy: one MIDI track creates the entire harmonic journey.

key_techniques: 8-bar vocal-only intro — creates maximum intimacy before any instrument enters
Harmonic chord loop implied by bass alone — no chord instrument needed
16th-note hat velocity cycling [93, 110, 127, 113] creates swinging groove without groove pool setting
Two bass voices (Organ Bass + FM Bass) cover different sections — Organ in break, FM in drops
Snare 1 fills (16th-note rolls) used as precise bar-transition triggers
Pads built up in three layers through the break (Pad 3 → Pad 1 → Pad 2) for progressive build
Lead doubles Pluck melody in final drop for extra weight without new notes
FX 5 drone sustains through bars 57–64 bridging break to final drop
theory_notes: Kisses is built on D natural minor at 142 BPM — the highest BPM of the tracks in this library. The chord loop Dm→F→Bb→Gm (i→bIII→bVI→iv) is all implied by the bass line — no chord instrument plays block chords. Analytically: i (Dm) = home, bIII (F major) = emotional lift, bVI (Bb major) = the peak of warmth, iv (Gm) = gentle pull back. This exact progression (or variations) appears throughout R&B, neo-soul, and UK garage because each step creates forward momentum without needing to 'resolve' — it just loops. The Pluck melody outlines these changes explicitly by landing on the root and 5th of each implied chord. At 142 BPM this feels urgent rather than melancholic — faster BPM transforms the same minor progression into something more driven.

elements_to_transplant: i→bIII→bVI→iv bass-implied chord loop (works in any minor key)
Vocal-only intro (8+ bars) for maximum intimacy
16th-note hat velocity cycling [93, 110, 127, 113] for swing without groove pool
Two-bass-voice technique (Organ Bass in breaks, FM Bass in drops)
Pluck melody that outlines bass-implied chords note by note
